devanagari: इक्ष्वाकुवंशवर्णनं तत्र मान्धातुश्चरितं सौभर्युपाख्यानं च । श्रीशुक उवाच । विरूपः केतुमान् शंभुः अंबरीषसुतास्त्रयः । विरूपात्पृषदश्वोऽभूत...
english_devnagari: ikṣvākuvaṃśavarṇanaṃ tatra māndhātuścaritaṃ saubharyupākhyānaṃ ca śrīśuka uvāca virūpaḥ ketumān śaṃbhuḥ aṃbarīṣasutāstrayaḥ virūpātpṛṣadaśvo

[nominative plural from √ sū class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ sū class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ sū class 2 verb]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Virūpa, Ketumān and Śambhu were the three sons of Ambarīṣa. From Virūpa was born Pṛṣadaśva whose son was Rathītara.

devanagari: रथीतरस्याप्रजस्य भार्यायां तन्तवेऽर्थितः । अङ्गिरा जनयामास ब्रह्मवर्चस्विनः सुतान् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: rathītarasyāprajasya bhāryāyāṃ tantave aṅgirā janayāmāsa brahmavarcasvinaḥ sutān
translation: When requested for the continuation of the family of Rathītara, Aṅgiras begot by the wife of Rathītara who was childless, a number of sons, possessing the lustre of the Vedic lore.

devanagari: एते क्षेत्रप्रसूता वै पुनस्त्वाङ्गिरसाः स्मृताः । रथीतराणां प्रवराः क्षत्रोपेता द्विजातयः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: ete kṣetraprasūtā vai punastvāṅgirasāḥ smṛtāḥ rathītarāṇāṃ pravarāḥ kṣatropetā dvijātayaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[nominative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb]

translation: Though these were born from the wife of Rathītara, they were known as belonging to the family of Aṅgiras (instead of that of Rathītara); they became the leaders of other sons of Rathītara as they were Brāhmaṇas endowed with the characteristic qualities of Kṣattriyas.

devanagari: क्षुवतस्तु मनोर्जज्ञे इक्ष्वाकुर्घ्राणतः सुतः । तस्य पुत्रशतज्येष्ठा विकुक्षिनिमिदण्डकाः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: kṣuvatastu manorjajñe ikṣvākurghrāṇataḥ sutaḥ tasya putraśatajyeṣṭhā vikukṣinimidaṇḍakāḥ

[perfect active second plural], [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: While Manu was sneezing his son Ikṣvāku was born from his nostrils. Of the hundred sons of Ikṣvāku, Vikukṣi, Nimi and Daṇḍaka were the eldest.

devanagari: तेषां पुरस्ताद् अभवन् आर्यावर्ते नृपा नृप । पञ्चविंशतिः पश्चाच्च त्रयो मध्ये परेऽन्यतः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: teṣāṃ purastād abhavan āryāvarte nṛpā nṛpa pañcaviṃśatiḥ paścācca trayo madhye pare
translation: Of these one hundred sons, twenty-five became monarchs in (the principalities lying in) the east of Āryāvarta[1], twenty-five became rulers in the West (of Āryāvarta), three in the middle (of that tract), and the rest in other directions (north, south and other directions).
purport:
Footnote [1]:
The sacred land between the Vindhya and the Himālayas—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā

devanagari: स एकदाष्टकाश्राद्धे इक्ष्वाकुः सुतमादिशत् । मांसमानीयतां मेध्यं विकुक्षे गच्छ मा चिरम् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: sa ekadāṣṭakāśrāddhe ikṣvākuḥ sutamādiśat māṃsamānīyatāṃ medhyaṃ vikukṣe gaccha mā ciram

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ su class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ su class 5 verb]

[nominative single from √ midh class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ midh class 1 verb]

translation: Once, on the occasion of aṣṭaka-śrāddha[2] Ikṣvāku ordered his son, “Oh Vikukṣi! Hurry up and bring meat suitable for the purpose of Śrāddha. Don’t delay, please.”
purport:
Footnote [2]:
(A Śrāddha to be performed on the seventh, eighth, ninth and the thirteenth day of dark and bright halfs of the lunar months).
Anvitārthaprakāśikā quotes:
saptamyādi-trayaṃ caiva tathā caiva trayodaśī!
catasras tvaṣṭakāḥ proktāḥ sarvapakṣād viśeṣataḥ!!

devanagari: तथेति स वनं गत्वा मृगान् हत्वा क्रियार्हणान् । श्रान्तो बुभुक्षितो वीरः शशं चाददपस्मृतिः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: tatheti sa vanaṃ gatvā mṛgān hatvā kriyārhaṇān śrānto bubhukṣito vīraḥ śaśaṃ cādadapasmṛtiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ śrā class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ śrā class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ śrā class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ śrā class 2 verb]

translation: Saying ‘Be it so’ (in compliance of his father’s order), the hero went to the forest, and killed beasts suitable for religious rites. Being fatigued and hungry, he unwittingly ate a hare.

devanagari: शेषं निवेदयामास पित्रे तेन च तद्‍गुरुः । चोदितः प्रोक्षणायाह दुष्टमेतद् अकर्मकम् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: śeṣaṃ nivedayāmāsa pitre tena ca tad‍guruḥ coditaḥ prokṣaṇāyāha duṣṭametad akarmakam
translation: He brought the remaining flesh to his father. When Ikṣvāku requested the family preceptor (Vasiṣṭha) to sprinkle it with water, he refused saying that it was contaminated, and hence unsuitable for religious rites.

devanagari: ज्ञात्वा पुत्रस्य तत्कर्म गुरुणाभिहितं नृपः । देशान्निःसारयामास सुतं त्यक्तविधिं रुषा ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: jñātvā putrasya tatkarma guruṇābhihitaṃ nṛpaḥ deśānniḥsārayāmāsa sutaṃ tyaktavidhiṃ ruṣā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ su class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ su class 5 verb]
[nominative single from √ sū class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ sū class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ sū class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ sū class 2 verb]

translation: Having ascertained from his son the perpetration of the (sacrilegious) act as reported by his family preceptor the king, out of anger, exiled from his land, his son who violated the restrictions of the rite.

devanagari: स तु विप्रेण संवादं जापकेन समाचरन् । त्यक्त्वा कलेवरं योगी स तेनावाप यत् परम् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: sa tu vipreṇa saṃvādaṃ jāpakena samācaran tyaktvā kalevaraṃ yogī sa tenāvāpa yat param

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: The king discussed with his preceptor Vasiṣṭha about the nature of the Truth. As a consequence of it, Ikṣvāku became established in Jñāna-yoga (path of knowledge). And casting off this physical body, he attained to what was the highest (Brahman).

devanagari: पितर्युपरतेऽभ्येत्य विकुक्षिः पृथिवीमिमाम् । शासदीजे हरिं यज्ञैः शशाद इति विश्रुतः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: pitaryuparate śāsadīje hariṃ yajñaiḥ śaśāda iti viśrutaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Vikukṣi who came to be known as Śaśāda (the eater of a hare) returned after the demise of his father, and while ruling over the earth, he worshipped Lord Hari with (the performance) of sacrifices.

devanagari: पुरञ्जयस्तस्य सुत इन्द्रवाह इतीरितः । ककुत्स्थ इति चाप्युक्तः शृणु नामानि कर्मभिः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: purañjayastasya suta indravāha itīritaḥ kakutstha iti cāpyuktaḥ śṛṇu nāmāni karmabhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vac class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ vac class 3 verb]

translation: His son Purañjaya (conqueror of a city) was called Indravāha (one borne by Indra), and was also named as Kakutstha (one seated on the hump of a bull). Now listen to (what) deeds earned for him these designations.

devanagari: कृतान्त आसीत् समरो देवानां सह दानवैः । पार्ष्णिग्राहो वृतो वीरो देवैर्दैत्यपराजितैः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛtānta āsīt samaro devānāṃ saha dānavaiḥ pārṣṇigrāho vṛto vīro devairdaityaparājitaiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [aorist active third single], [injunctive active third single]

[instrumental plural from √ rāj class 1 verb], [instrumental plural from √ rāj ]

translation: At the end of the Kṛta Age (or at the beginning of the Tretā age)[3], there was a battle between gods and Dānavas—a battle that was fierce like the Pralaya, at the end of world. This hero (Purañjaya) was sought as an ally by gods who were defeated by Daityas.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Kṛtānta—Both the meanings of the pun on Kṛtānta are accepted.

devanagari: वचनाद् देवदेवस्य विष्णोर्विश्वात्मनः प्रभोः । वाहनत्वे वृतस्तस्य बभूवेन्द्रो महावृषः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: vacanād devadevasya viṣṇorviśvātmanaḥ prabhoḥ vāhanatve vṛtastasya babhūvendro mahāvṛṣaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: (‘I shall kill the demons if Indra agrees to be my mount’ on this condition) Indra was sought to be a mount. According to the advice of Lord Viṣṇu, the god of gods and the soul of the universe, Indra assumed the farm of a big bull (to carry Purañjaya).

devanagari: स सन्नद्धो धनुर्दिव्यं आदाय विशिखान्छितान् । स्तूयमानः समारुह्य युयुत्सुः ककुदि स्थितः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: sa sannaddho dhanurdivyaṃ ādāya viśikhānchitān stūyamānaḥ samāruhya yuyutsuḥ kakudi sthitaḥ
translation: Accoutered in a coat of mail and armed with a celestial bow and sharp arrows, he, being eager to fight, mounted the bull and occupied his seat on its hump, while he was being extolled by gods.

devanagari: तेजसाऽप्यायितो विष्णोः पुरुषस्य परात्मनः । प्रतीच्यां दिशि दैत्यानां न्यरुणत् त्रिदशैः पुरम् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: tejasā pratīcyāṃ diśi daityānāṃ nyaruṇat tridaśaiḥ puram

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second single], [imperfect active third single]

translation: Strengthened with the spiritual energy of Lord Viṣṇu, the Inner Controller, the Supreme Soul, he besieged the town of the Daityas in the west, with the army of gods.

devanagari: तैस्तस्य चाभूत् प्रधनं तुमुलं लोमहर्षणम् । यमाय भल्लैरनयद् दैत्यान् येऽभिययुर्मृधे ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: taistasya cābhūt pradhanaṃ tumulaṃ lomaharṣaṇam yamāya bhallairanayad daityān ye
translation: He engaged them in a fierce battle which made hair stand on end. Whosoever of the Daityas dared to face in the battle, he despatched them to the god of death with his bhalla arrows.

devanagari: तस्येषुपाताभिमुखं युगान्ताग्निं इवोल्बणम् । विसृज्य दुद्रुवुर्दैत्या हन्यमानाः स्वमालयम् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyeṣupātābhimukhaṃ yugāntāgniṃ ivolbaṇam visṛjya dudruvurdaityā hanyamānāḥ svamālayam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [present passive first single], [imperative active second single]
[nominative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ han class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ han class 2 verb]

[nominative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ han class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ han class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ han class 2 verb]

translation: While the Daityas were being killed, they avoided the range of the flight of his arrows which were unbearable like the fire that consumes the universe at the end of yugas and fled to their region (the subterranean world).

devanagari: जित्वा परं धनं सर्वं सश्रीकं वज्रपाणये । प्रत्ययच्छत्स राजर्षिः इति नामभिराहृतः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: jitvā paraṃ dhanaṃ sarvaṃ saśrīkaṃ vajrapāṇaye pratyayacchatsa rājarṣiḥ iti nāmabhirāhṛtaḥ
translation: Having conquered the splendid city and all its wealth, the royal sage gave it all to Indra, the wielder of the thunderbolt. Hence he was designated with the epithets (mentioned above).

devanagari: पुरञ्जयस्य पुत्रोऽभूद् अनेनास्तत्सुतः पृथुः । विश्वगन्धिस्ततश्चन्द्रो युवनाश्वस्तु तत्सुतः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: purañjayasya putro viśvagandhistataścandro yuvanāśvastu tatsutaḥ
translation: Purañjaya had a son called Anenas (the sinless). His son was Pṛthu whose son was Viśvarandhi (v.l. Viśvagandha) His son was Candra whose son was Yuvanāśva.

devanagari: शाबस्तः तत्सुतो येन शाबस्ती निर्ममे पुरी । बृहदश्वस्तु शाबस्तिः ततः कुवलयाश्वकः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: śābastaḥ tatsuto yena śābastī nirmame purī bṛhadaśvastu śābastiḥ tataḥ kuvalayāśvakaḥ
translation: His son was Śābasta who built the city called Śābasti. Bṛhadaśva was the son of Śābasta; thence(the next generation) was Kuvalayāśva.

devanagari: यः प्रियार्थमुतंकस्य धुन्धुनामासुरं बली । सुतानां एकविंशत्या सहस्रैः अहनद् वृतः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: yaḥ priyārthamutaṃkasya dhundhunāmāsuraṃ balī sutānāṃ ekaviṃśatyā sahasraiḥ ahanad vṛtaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]

translation: For achieving the well-being of sage Utaṅka, the powerful king, surrrounded by his twenty-one thousand sons, killed the demon called Dhundhu.

devanagari: धुन्धुमार इति ख्यातः तत्सुतास्ते च जज्वलुः । धुन्धोर्मुखाग्निना सर्वे त्रय एवावशेषिताः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: dhundhumāra iti khyātaḥ tatsutāste ca jajvaluḥ dhundhormukhāgninā sarve traya evāvaśeṣitāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ khyā class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ khyā class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ khyā class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ khyā class 2 verb]

[nominative plural from √ sū class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ sū class 6 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sū class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ sū class 6 verb], [accusative plural from √ sū class 6 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sū class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ sū class 2 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sū class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ sū class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ sū class 2 verb]

translation: He, thereby, became celebrated as Dhundhumāra. All of his sons except the three who survived, were burnt by the fire that emanated from the mouth of Dhundhu.

devanagari: दृढाश्वः कपिलाश्वश्च भद्राश्व इति भारत । दृढाश्वपुत्रो हर्यश्वो निकुम्भः तत्सुतः स्मृतः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: dṛḍhāśvaḥ kapilāśvaśca bhadrāśva iti bhārata dṛḍhāśvaputro haryaśvo nikumbhaḥ tatsutaḥ smṛtaḥ
translation: The surviving sons were Dṛḍhāśva, Kapilāśva and Bhadrāśva, Oh descendant of Bharata. Dṛḍhāśva’s son was Haryaśva whose son is remembered at Nikumbha.

devanagari: बहुलाश्वो निकुम्भस्य कृशाश्वोऽथास्य सेनजित् । युवनाश्वोऽभवत्तस्य सोऽनपत्यो वनं गतः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: bahulāśvo nikumbhasya kṛśāśvo yuvanāśvo
translation: Barhaṇāśva was the son of Nikumbha. His (Barhaṇāśva’s) son was Kṛśāśva whose son was Senajit. To him was born Yuvanāśva who, being childless, repaired to the forest, along with his hundred wives, in despair. The merciful sages took pity on him, and with great concentration of mind, they conducted for him a sacrifice, specially dedicated to Indra.

devanagari: भार्याशतेन निर्विण्ण ऋषयोऽस्य कृपालवः । इष्टिं स्म वर्तयां चक्रुः ऐन्द्रीं ते सुसमाहिताः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: bhāryāśatena nirviṇṇa ṛṣayo iṣṭiṃ sma vartayāṃ cakruḥ aindrīṃ te susamāhitāḥ
translation: Barhaṇāśva was the son of Nikumbha. His (Barhaṇāśva’s) son was Kṛśāśva whose son was Senajit. To him was born Yuvanāśva who, being childless, repaired to the forest, along with his hundred wives, in despair. The merciful sages took pity on him, and with great concentration of mind, they conducted for him a sacrifice, specially dedicated to Indra.

devanagari: राजा तद् यज्ञसदनं प्रविष्टो निशि तर्षितः । दृष्ट्वा शयानान् विप्रांस्तान् पपौ मंत्रजलं स्वयम् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: rājā tad yajñasadanaṃ praviṣṭo niśi tarṣitaḥ dṛṣṭvā śayānān viprāṃstān papau maṃtrajalaṃ svayam

translation: (Once) being thirsty at night, the king entered the sacrificial hall. Finding all the Brāhmaṇas in the hall asleep, he himself drank the water consecrated with mantras (and meant for the queen).

devanagari: उत्थितास्ते निशम्याथ व्युदकं कलशं प्रभो । पप्रच्छुः कस्य कर्मेदं पीतं पुंसवनं जलम् ॥ २८ ॥

translation: It is reported that after the expiry of the due period (of nine months), the son of Yuvanāśva who (later) became the sovereign monarch of the globe of the earth, was born splitting open the right side of his belly.

devanagari: कं धास्यति कुमारोऽयं स्तन्यं रोरूयते भृशम् । मां धाता वत्स मा रोदीः इतीन्द्रो देशिनीमदात् ॥ ३१ ॥

translation: ‘The child is incessantly crying[4]. Whom will it suck?’ (asked the Brāhmaṇas anxiously ‘He will suckle me. Oh child, do not cry; saying this, Indra (who was propitiated with a sacrifice for its birth) put (in his mouth) his index finger (which was dripping with nectar.)
purport:
Footnote [4]:
The child which wishes to suck the breast of his mother is incessantly crying—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: न ममार पिता तस्य विप्रदेवप्रसादतः । युवनाश्वोऽथ तत्रैव तपसा सिद्धिमन्वगात् ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: na mamāra pitā tasya vipradevaprasādataḥ yuvanāśvo

translation: Through the grace of gods and that of Brāhmaṇas, his (Māndhātṛ’s) father did not die. Yuvanāśva attained to the Final Beatitude, there (in that very hermitage) through his penance.

devanagari: त्रसद्दस्युरितीन्द्रोऽङ्ग विदधे नाम यस्य वै । यस्मात् त्रसन्ति हि उद्विग्ना दस्यवो रावणादयः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: trasaddasyuritīndro yasmāt trasanti hi udvignā dasyavo rāvaṇādayaḥ

translation: Indra gave him Trasad-dasyu (The terror of evildoers) as another epithet. For enemies of gods and men like Rāvaṇa and others trembled at the mention of his name and were perturbed.

devanagari: यौवनाश्वोऽथ मान्धाता चक्रवर्त्यवनीं प्रभुः । सप्तद्वीपवतीमेकः शशासाच्युततेजसा ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: yauvanāśvo saptadvīpavatīmekaḥ śaśāsācyutatejasā

translation: In due course, Māndhātā, the son of Yuvanāśva became the universal monarch, and being infused with the Spiritual power and glory of the Imperishable Lord (Viṣṇu), solely ruled over the world consisting of seven island-continents.

devanagari: ईजे च यज्ञं क्रतुभिः आत्मविद् भूरिदक्षिणैः । सर्वदेवमयं देवं सर्वात्मकमतीन्द्रियम् ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: īje ca yajñaṃ kratubhiḥ ātmavid bhūridakṣiṇaiḥ sarvadevamayaṃ devaṃ sarvātmakamatīndriyam

translation: A knower of the Self as he was, he performed a number of sacrifices with liberal gifts and dakṣiṇās, and thereby worshipped the Supreme Deity of the sacrifices, the Lord who represents all gods (in his person) and who, through his being the inner Controller of all antaryāmins is beyond the ken of senses.

devanagari: द्रव्यं मंत्रो विधिर्यज्ञो यजमानस्तथर्त्विजः । धर्मो देशश्च कालश्च सर्वं एतद् यदात्मकम् ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: dravyaṃ maṃtro vidhiryajño yajamānastathartvijaḥ dharmo deśaśca kālaśca sarvaṃ etad yadātmakam
translation: Materials of sacrifices, mantras (recited in the course of the sacrifice), the sacrificial procedure, the sacrifice proper, the performer of sacrifice and priests participating in the sacrifice, the merit accruing from the sacrifice, time and place of sacrifice—all these are his constituents. (In fact, the whole of the universe is constituted of him. Him he worshipped through sacrifices.

devanagari: यावत् सूर्य उदेति स्म यावच्च प्रतितिष्ठति । सर्वं तत् यौवनाश्वस्य मान्धातुः क्षेत्रमुच्यते ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: yāvat sūrya udeti sma yāvacca pratitiṣṭhati sarvaṃ tat yauvanāśvasya māndhātuḥ kṣetramucyate

translation: The point at which the sun rises, to the point where it sets—the whole of the region lighted by the sun is regarded as the land (kingdom) of Māndhātṛ, the son of Yuvanāśva.

devanagari: शशबिन्दोर्दुहितरि बिन्दुमत्यामधान् नृपः । पुरुकुत्सं अंबरीषं मुचुकुन्दं च योगिनम् । तेषां स्वसारः पञ्चाशत् सौभरिं वव्रिरे पतिम् ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: śaśabindorduhitari bindumatyāmadhān nṛpaḥ purukutsaṃ aṃbarīṣaṃ mucukundaṃ ca yoginam teṣāṃ svasāraḥ pañcāśat saubhariṃ vavrire patim

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active third single], [imperfect active third plural], [aorist active third single]

translation: The king begot on Bindumatī, the daughter of Śaśabindu, (three sons, viz.) Purukutsa, Ambarīṣa and Mucakunda, the yogin. Their fifty sisters sought the sage Saubhari[5] as their husband.
purport:
Footnote [5]:
Saubhari—The story, how the old sage Śaubhari married all the fifty daughters of the emperor Māndhātṛ seems to be popular in the epic period, and is found in Viṣṇu Purāṇa. 4.2.3, Garuḍa Purāṇa. 1.138. In fact the whole of that chapter seems to be based on this chapter of the Bhāgavata Purāṇa

devanagari: यमुनान्तर्जले मग्नः तप्यमानः परंतपः । निर्वृतिं मीनराजस्य दृष्ट्वा मैथुनधर्मिणः ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: yamunāntarjale magnaḥ tapyamānaḥ paraṃtapaḥ nirvṛtiṃ mīnarājasya dṛṣṭvā maithunadharmiṇaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ tap class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ tap class 4 verb]

translation: Submerged in the waters of the Yamunā, and practising austere penance, Saubhari happened to observe the happy state of the king of fishes engaged in coition (with a female partner), and he was affected with that passionate desire, and (approaching the king) requested him for a girl. (The king) replied. “Oh Brahman, you can freely have any daughter, provided she selects you of her own accord voluntarily.

devanagari: जातस्पृहो नृपं विप्रः कन्यां एकां अयाचत । सोऽप्याह गृह्यतां ब्रह्मन् कामं कन्या स्वयंवरे ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: jātaspṛho nṛpaṃ vipraḥ kanyāṃ ekāṃ ayācata so

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second plural], [imperfect middle third single]

translation: Submerged in the waters of the Yamunā, and practising austere penance, Saubhari happened to observe the happy state of the king of fishes engaged in coition (with a female partner), and he was affected with that passionate desire, and (approaching the king) requested him for a girl. (The king) replied. “Oh Brahman, you can freely have any daughter, provided she selects you of her own accord voluntarily.

devanagari: स विचिन्त्याप्रियं स्त्रीणां जरठोऽयं असम्मतः । वलीपलित एजत्क इत्यहं प्रत्युदाहृतः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: sa vicintyāpriyaṃ strīṇāṃ jaraṭho valīpalita ejatka ityahaṃ pratyudāhṛtaḥ
translation: The sage realized the ruse and said to himself, ‘Considering that I, being far advanced in age, grey-haired, covered with wrinkles, with a head unstable and shaking (all the while), will be repulsive and disagreeable to women, I have been (politely) refused by the king.

devanagari: साधयिष्ये तथात्मानं सुरस्त्रीणामभीप्सितम् । किं पुनर्मनुजेन्द्राणां इति व्यवसितः प्रभुः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: sādhayiṣye tathātmānaṃ surastrīṇāmabhīpsitam kiṃ punarmanujendrāṇāṃ iti vyavasitaḥ prabhuḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ man class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 8 verb]
[nominative single from √ man class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ man class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 8 verb]

translation: I shall make myself so (attractively) beautiful that I shall be covetable even to celestial nymphs; not to speak of daughters of lords of human beings. The spiritual master (Saubhari) became thus determined (and by his power of asceticism, he became so actually).

devanagari: मुनिः प्रवेशितः क्षत्त्रा कन्यान्तःपुरमृद्धिमत् । वृतः स राजकन्याभिः एकं पञ्चाशता वरः ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: muniḥ praveśitaḥ kṣattrā kanyāntaḥpuramṛddhimat vṛtaḥ sa rājakanyābhiḥ ekaṃ pañcāśatā varaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]

translation: He was ushered in by the chamberlain, in the richly magnificent and luxurious harem reserved for the princesses. And Lo! That one suitor was sought after and courted as a husband by all the fifty princesses.

devanagari: तासां कलिरभूद् भूयान् तत् अर्थेऽपोह्य सौहृदम् । ममानुरूपो नायं व इति तद्‍गतचेतसाम् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: tāsāṃ kalirabhūd bhūyān tat arthe mamānurūpo nāyaṃ va iti tad‍gatacetasām
translation: Throwing to winds (forgetting) their mutual sisterly affection, there arose a great controversy among them about securing him (as a husband). Their hearts being fixed upon him, each one of them argued, “He is suitable in beauty and form only to me, and not to anyone of you (at all)”.

devanagari: स बह्वृचस्ताभिरपारणीय । तपःश्रियानर्घ्यपरिच्छदेषु । गृहेषु नानोपवनामलाम्भः । सरःसु सौगन्धिककाननेषु ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: sa bahvṛcastābhirapāraṇīya tapaḥśriyānarghyaparicchadeṣu gṛheṣu nānopavanāmalāmbhaḥ saraḥsu saugandhikakānaneṣu
translation: Saubhari, well-versed in the Ṛgveda (and master of powerful mantras) enjoyed for a long period his life with them (the princesses of Māndhātṛ) in mansions furnished by the power of his inexhaustible penance, with invaluable paraphernalia and furnishings such as highly costly beds, seats, rich clothes, ornaments, baths, articles of beautifications (pigments etc.), sweet dishes, wreaths and flowers, waited upon by men and women adorned with precious ornaments, and resonant with warbling birds, humming bees and singing panegyrists. He revelled with them delightfully in various gardens studded with pools of translucent waters, and in parks full of scented flowers (like Kalhāra).

devanagari: महार्हशय्यासनवस्त्रभूषण । स्नानानुलेपाभ्यवहारमाल्यकैः । स्वलङ्कृतस्त्रीपुरुषेषु नित्यदा । रेमेऽनुगायद् द्विजभृङ्गवन्दिषु ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: mahārhaśayyāsanavastrabhūṣaṇa snānānulepābhyavahāramālyakaiḥ svalaṅkṛtastrīpuruṣeṣu nityadā reme

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Saubhari, well-versed in the Ṛgveda (and master of powerful mantras) enjoyed for a long period his life with them (the princesses of Māndhātṛ) in mansions furnished by the power of his inexhaustible penance, with invaluable paraphernalia and furnishings such as highly costly beds, seats, rich clothes, ornaments, baths, articles of beautifications (pigments etc.), sweet dishes, wreaths and flowers, waited upon by men and women adorned with precious ornaments, and resonant with warbling birds, humming bees and singing panegyrists. He revelled with them delightfully in various gardens studded with pools of translucent waters, and in parks full of scented flowers (like Kalhāra).

devanagari: यद्‍गार्हस्थ्यं तु संवीक्ष्य सप्तद्वीपवतीपतिः । विस्मितः स्तम्भमजहात् सार्वभौमश्रियान्वितम् ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: yad‍gārhasthyaṃ tu saṃvīkṣya saptadvīpavatīpatiḥ vismitaḥ stambhamajahāt sārvabhaumaśriyānvitam
translation: Observing his affluent state of house-holder’s life, Māndhātā, the master of the entire earth consisting of seven island-continents, was so astonished that he ceased to feel the obstinate pride of universal sovereignty.

devanagari: एवं गृहेष्वभिरतो विषयान् विविधैः सुखैः । सेवमानो न चातुष्यद् आज्यस्तोकैरिवानलः ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ gṛheṣvabhirato viṣayān vividhaiḥ sukhaiḥ sevamāno na cātuṣyad ājyastokairivānalaḥ
translation: Although he was enjoying various objects of pleasure with deep attachment to his houses which were full of various kinds of pleasure[6], Saubhari did not feel sated like a fire fed with drops of ghee.
purport:
Footnote [6]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā reads nānā-mukhaiḥ: ‘Through many sets of senseorgans’. Saubhari assumed various bodies simultaneously to enjoy through multiple sets of sense-organs, all the objects of pleasure fully.

devanagari: स कदाचिद् उपासीन आत्मापह्नवमात्मनः । ददर्श बह्वृचाचार्यो मीनसङ्गसमुत्थितम् ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: sa kadācid upāsīna ātmāpahnavamātmanaḥ dadarśa bahvṛcācāryo mīnasaṅgasamutthitam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: On one occasion, while he was sitting (all alone), Saubhari, the great preceptor of Ṛgveda, became conscious of his ascetic degeneration (spiritual oblivion) caused by his observation of the sexual pleasure of the fish.

devanagari: अहो इमं पश्यत मे विनाशं । तपस्विनः सच्चरितव्रतस्य । अन्तर्जले वारिचरप्रसङ्गात् । प्रच्यावितं ब्रह्म चिरं धृतं यत् ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: aho imaṃ paśyata me vināśaṃ tapasvinaḥ saccaritavratasya antarjale vāricaraprasaṅgāt pracyāvitaṃ brahma ciraṃ dhṛtaṃ yat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: ‘Alas! Look at the ruinous fall of mine—an ascetic of pious conduct, conforming strictly to vows, due to my noticing (the sexual intercourse of) the acquatic animals, under waters, my asceticism[7] which I observed for a long time, was lost.’
purport:
Footnote [7]:
brahma—Brahman, the Supreme Spirit I used to meditate upon for a long time past, was completely forgotten—Bhāgavata Candrikā

devanagari: सङ्गं त्यजेत मिथुनव्रतीनां मुमुक्षुः । सर्वात्मना न विसृजेद् बहिरिन्द्रियाणि । एकश्चरन् रहसि चित्तमनन्त ईशे । युञ्जीत तद्व्रतिषु साधुषु ...
english_devnagari: saṅgaṃ tyajeta mithunavratīnāṃ mumukṣuḥ sarvātmanā na visṛjed bahirindriyāṇi ekaścaran rahasi cittamananta īśe yuñjīta tadvratiṣu sādhuṣu cet prasaṅgaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: A person desirous of liberation from Saṃsāra, should, with all his heart and soul, avoid association with all who follow married life. He should endeavour not to permit his senses to external objects. Leading a life in solitude, he should fix his mind on the Infinite Ruler (of the universe). If occasion arises, he should associate himself with saintly persons who observe vows (of celibacy etc.) and are devoted to the Lord.

devanagari: एकस्तपस्व्यहमथाम्भसि मत्स्यसङ्गात् । पञ्चाशदासमुत पञ्चसहस्रसर्गः । नान्तं व्रजाम्युभयकृत्यमनोरथानां । मायागुणैः हृतमतिर्विषयेऽर्थभावः ॥ ...
english_devnagari: ekastapasvyahamathāmbhasi matsyasaṅgāt pañcāśadāsamuta pañcasahasrasargaḥ nāntaṃ vrajāmyubhayakṛtyamanorathānāṃ māyāguṇaiḥ hṛtamatirviṣaye

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

[nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]
[nominative single from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ hṛ class 1 verb]

translation: I was an ascetic without any companion. But due to my association with the fish under water, I became fifty (by marrying fifty wives[8]). (By procreating hundred sons per wife) I became five thousand. I cannot reach the end of desires about them, or I do not see any limit to my wishes relating to man and wife, or this world and the next. I have lost my judgment due to the guṇas of Māyā. I have come to believe that achievement of worldly objects are the real goal in life.
purport:
Footnote [8]:
Anvitārthaprakāśikā: I assumed fifty forms for simultaneous association with fifty wives.

devanagari: एवं वसन् गृहे कालं विरक्तो न्यासमास्थितः । वनं जगाम अनुययुः तत्पत्‍न्यः पतिदेवताः ॥ ५३ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ vasan gṛhe kālaṃ virakto nyāsamāsthitaḥ vanaṃ jagāma anuyayuḥ tatpat‍nyaḥ patidevatāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vas class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ vas class 1 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Continuing in the householder’s stage of life for some (more) time, he felt disinclination (to that life), and adopted the life of a Vānaprastha (nyāsa). He repaired to the forest and was followed by his fifty wives who looked upon their husband as God.

devanagari: तत्र तप्त्वा तपस्तीक्ष्णं आत्मदर्शनमात्मवान् । सहैवाग्निभिरात्मानं युयोज परमात्मनि ॥ ५४ ॥
english_devnagari: tatra taptvā tapastīkṣṇaṃ ātmadarśanamātmavān sahaivāgnibhirātmānaṃ yuyoja paramātmani

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [imperative middle first single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: The self-controlled sage performed there a severely austere penance which reduced his body (to a skeleton), and got absorbed along with the fires (like gārhapatya) into the Supreme Soul (and was liberated from saṃsāra).

devanagari: ताः स्वपत्युर्महाराज निरीक्ष्याध्यात्मिकीं गतिम् । अन्वीयुस्तत्प्रभावेण अग्निं शान्तमिवार्चिषः ॥ ५५ ॥
english_devnagari: tāḥ svapatyurmahārāja nirīkṣyādhyātmikīṃ gatim anvīyustatprabhāveṇa agniṃ śāntamivārciṣaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ svap class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ svap class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ svap class 2 verb], [locative single from √ svap class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ svap class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ svap class 2 verb]
[accusative single from √ śam class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ śam class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ śam class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ śam class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ śam class 9 verb], [accusative single from √ śam class 9 verb]

translation:
Noticing the absorption of their husband in the Supreme Brahman, his wives too followed him through his spiritual power (and attained Liberation from Saṃsāra) just as flames become extinguished with a subsided fire.[9]
